status_notes: Project momentum declined after McAfee → Trellix transition; track if community activity revives or if Trellix moves the official site.
notes: OpenDXL is a publish/subscribe messaging fabric for security tool integration, originally open-sourced by McAfee (now Trellix). Defines a wire protocol and ontology so disparate security products can exchange events, request-response messages, and brokered topic data without point-to-point custom integrations. A data-exchange/integration spec — placed under reference per the methodology-vs-reference distinction (the value is in the wire format and ontology, not in standalone judgement guidance).

data.description: Publish/subscribe messaging fabric and ontology for security-tool integration. Defines a broker-based wire protocol over MQTT/AMQP plus a message ontology covering events, queries, and responses. Multiple language SDKs (Python, Java, C, JavaScript).
data.notes: Originally open-sourced by McAfee under the Open DXL initiative. Active community development was strong 2016-2020; momentum declined after the McAfee Enterprise → Trellix transition (2022). The github.com/opendxl organization remains active but commit cadence has slowed. Worth re-checking project status before citing in long-term plans.
